steps.title: Use the cost contrast
steps.content: Radar and sonar can work through walls, but the passage says they are too costly for consumer use. The Wi-Fi alternative is then described as a cheap way to do a similar task, so the blank must point to lower cost.

steps.title: Reject other qualities
steps.content: A describes frequency, not cost. C describes clarity or certainty, which is not the basis of comparison. D says impractical, the opposite of a cheap consumer solution. The remaining choice directly contrasts with too costly. The answer is B.
